known_issues.title: Engine knocking / connecting rod bearing wear
known_issues.description: Some GDI engines from this era develop rod bearing wear that produces a knocking noise and can lead to stalling, part of a broader Hyundai engine recall and extended warranty program.
known_issues.symptoms: A deep knocking noise from the engine at idle that gets louder under load, sometimes accompanied by the check engine light or a sudden stall while driving.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask whether the vehicle was inspected under Hyundai's Knock Sensor Detection System recall and listen closely for engine knock at idle.

known_issues.title: AC compressor failure
known_issues.description: The AC compressor clutch can seize or wear out prematurely, causing intermittent cooling loss and a rattling noise.
known_issues.symptoms: AC that blows warm intermittently, a rattling or grinding noise from the compressor when the AC engages, and the compressor clutch not always kicking in.
known_issues.recommendation: Run the AC on max cold for several minutes to confirm consistent cooling.

known_issues.title: Sunroof rattle
known_issues.description: The sunroof track and wind deflector can develop a rattle over bumps as mounting clips loosen with age.
known_issues.symptoms: A rattling or buzzing noise from the roof area when driving over uneven pavement, most noticeable with the sunroof closed.
known_issues.recommendation: Open and close the sunroof and drive over uneven pavement to listen for rattling.

known_issues.title: Blind spot detection sensor malfunction
known_issues.description: The rear corner radar sensors can throw intermittent faults, disabling blind spot warning until the vehicle is restarted.
known_issues.symptoms: A blind spot monitoring warning light or dash message indicating the system is temporarily unavailable, with the feature not reactivating until restart.
known_issues.recommendation: Check the dash for a blind spot warning fault light during the test drive.

generations.label: 3rd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4, Mazda CX-5

known_issues.title: Engine failure from connecting rod bearing wear
known_issues.description: 2.0L and 2.4L Theta II GDI engines in this generation are covered under Hyundai's engine recall program for premature rod bearing wear that can cause knocking, stalling, or a seized engine.
known_issues.symptoms: A deep knocking noise from the engine that intensifies under load or at idle, sometimes followed by a sudden stall or the engine seizing entirely.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask for recall and warranty records confirming engine inspection or replacement, and listen for knocking at idle and under load.

known_issues.title: Panoramic sunroof spontaneous shattering
known_issues.description: The large panoramic sunroof glass on higher trims has a documented history of cracking or shattering without impact, especially in temperature swings.
known_issues.symptoms: Visible spiderweb cracking or shattered glass in the sunroof panel with no obvious impact damage, sometimes preceded by a loud popping noise.
known_issues.recommendation: Inspect the sunroof glass closely for stress cracks around the edges.

known_issues.title: 7-speed dual-clutch transmission hesitation
known_issues.description: 1.6T models with the 7-speed DCT can exhibit hesitation or a jerky feel from a stop as the clutch packs wear.
known_issues.symptoms: A noticeable delay or jerky lurch when pulling away from a stop, especially pronounced on inclines or in stop-and-go traffic.
known_issues.recommendation: Test drive from a full stop repeatedly, including uphill, to feel for hesitation or shudder.

known_issues.title: AC condenser corrosion
known_issues.description: Road salt exposure corrodes the front-mounted AC condenser, leading to slow refrigerant leaks.
known_issues.symptoms: AC that gradually cools less effectively over time, eventually blowing warm air, sometimes with visible corrosion on the condenser fins.
known_issues.recommendation: Confirm AC blows consistently cold on a warm day before purchase.
